name: fal
description: Run hosted generative AI models synchronously or through the fal queue API

tools.name: Run Model
tools.description: Run a fal model synchronously and wait for the result (best for fast models; billed per run). The model endpoint ID is split into owner / app / variant segments, e.g. fal-ai/flux/schnell

app_owner.description: First segment of the model endpoint ID (the owner), e.g. fal-ai

app_id.description: Second segment of the model endpoint ID (the app), e.g. flux or fast-sdxl

app_variant.description: Third segment of the model endpoint ID when present (the variant), e.g. schnell or dev; leave empty for two-segment IDs like fal-ai/fast-sdxl

input.description: Model input payload sent as the request body, e.g. {"prompt": "a red panda"} — see the model's API page for its schema

tools.name: Submit Queue Request
tools.description: Submit a request to the fal queue and return immediately with a request_id (billed per run)

app_owner.description: First segment of the model endpoint ID (the owner), e.g. fal-ai

app_id.description: Second segment of the model endpoint ID (the app), e.g. flux or fast-sdxl

app_variant.description: Third segment of the model endpoint ID when present (the variant), e.g. schnell or dev; leave empty for two-segment IDs like fal-ai/fast-sdxl

input.description: Model input payload sent as the request body, e.g. {"prompt": "a red panda"} — see the model's API page for its schema

tools.name: Get Queue Status
tools.description: Check the status of a queued request (IN_QUEUE, IN_PROGRESS, or COMPLETED). Use the base app ID without the variant segment, matching the status_url returned at submit time

app_owner.description: Owner segment of the model endpoint ID, e.g. fal-ai

app_id.description: Base app segment without the variant (for fal-ai/flux/schnell use flux), matching the status_url returned by Submit Queue Request

request_id.description: Request ID returned by Submit Queue Request

logs.description: Set to 1 to include execution logs in the status response

tools.name: Get Queue Result
tools.description: Fetch the result of a completed queue request. Use the base app ID without the variant segment, matching the response_url returned at submit time

app_owner.description: Owner segment of the model endpoint ID, e.g. fal-ai

app_id.description: Base app segment without the variant (for fal-ai/flux/schnell use flux), matching the response_url returned by Submit Queue Request

request_id.description: Request ID returned by Submit Queue Request

tools.name: Cancel Queue Request
tools.description: Cancel a queued request that has not started running yet

app_owner.description: Owner segment of the model endpoint ID, e.g. fal-ai

app_id.description: Base app segment without the variant (for fal-ai/flux/schnell use flux)

request_id.description: Request ID returned by Submit Queue Request

prompts.title: Generate an image
prompts.prompt: Run the fal-ai/flux/schnell model on fal with a prompt I provide and show me the resulting image URL.

prompts.title: Queue a long job
prompts.prompt: Submit a request to a fal model via the queue, then poll its status and fetch the result when it completes.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: fal API Setup

steps.title: Create a fal account
steps.description: Sign up at https://fal.ai — new accounts get free credits to try models before adding billing.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open https://fal.ai/dashboard/keys, create a key, and copy it immediately — it is only shown once.

steps.title: Store the key
steps.description: Add it to your .env file as FAL_KEY=... — requests authenticate with the header 'Authorization: Key <FAL_KEY>'.

steps.title: Pick a model and verify
steps.description: Browse https://fal.ai/models and copy a model endpoint ID (e.g. fal-ai/flux/schnell). Pass its segments as app_owner=fal-ai, app_id=flux, app_variant=schnell to the Run Model tool with a small input payload.

notes: Each model has its own input schema — check the model's API tab on fal.ai/models for required fields
Use the queue tools (submit → status → result) for slow models like video generation; synchronous fal.run requests can time out
Queue status/result/cancel URLs use the base app ID without the variant segment (fal returns ready-made status_url and response_url in the submit response)
Model runs are billed per inference — check the model page for pricing
